description_long: This object consists of a group of three distinct flowering plants, each with unique characteristics. The first plant features tall, upright green stems with elongated, lanceolate leaves and white, feathery inflorescences resembling pampas grass. The second plant has a dense rosette of long, narrow, yellow-green leaves radiating outward, with a central stem that transitions from green at the base to a vivid red at the top, supporting spiky, red-tipped bracts. The third plant is a low, ground-hugging cluster of fine, delicate, pale green foliage, appearing almost moss-like. The overall arrangement suggests ornamental landscaping, suitable for urban parks or decorative planters. The colors range from deep greens and yellows to striking reds and soft whites, creating a visually dynamic composition. The surfaces appear semi-soft and matte, typical of living vegetation.
description: A group of three ornamental flowering plants: one with tall green stems and white feathery plumes, one with yellow-green spiky leaves and a red-tipped center, and a low cluster of fine pale green foliage.
description_view_0: The front view shows all three plants clearly, with the tall white-plumed plant and the red-tipped spiky plant most prominent, and the low pale green foliage visible at the base.
description_view_1: From this angle, the yellow-green spiky plant is in the foreground, partially obscuring the red-tipped center, while the white-plumed plant is seen from the side.
description_view_2: This view highlights the back of the red-tipped spiky plant and the side profile of the white-plumed plant, with the low foliage less visible.
description_view_3: The white-plumed plant is most prominent, with the yellow-green spiky plant to the right and the low pale green foliage clearly visible at the bottom.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals and bracts are arranged in a radial and whorled configuration, with the red-tipped plant displaying a dense, spiky rosette and the white-plumed plant showing feathery inflorescences in a radial pattern.
